stories.headline: Anthropic Overtakes OpenAI in U.S. Business Adoption for the First Time
stories.key_takeaway: For the first time, more U.S. businesses are paying for Anthropic's Claude than OpenAI's ChatGPT, driven by Claude Code's explosive growth, though IDC data shows Anthropic still trails on depth of enterprise use.

pull_quote.text: Anthropic has already been in the lead amongst the high adoption groups like finance, tech, professional services — it's across the other firms where OpenAI still has a lead, but that has been shrinking over the past couple of months.

data_points.label: Public GitHub commits authored by Claude Code

stories.article_excerpt: The numbers had been converging for months. In April, they finally crossed: Anthropic's share of U.S. business adoption pulled ahead of OpenAI's.

stories.headline: EngineAI Files Confidentially for a Hong Kong IPO as China's Humanoid Robot Race Accelerates
stories.key_takeaway: EngineAI, a two-year-old Shenzhen humanoid robot maker valued at $1.5 billion, has confidentially filed for a Hong Kong IPO just after opening a factory that can build a T800 robot every 15 minutes.

pull_quote.text: We've evolved from initial testing to small-scale production and now to a phase of mass delivery, marking a milestone in industrialization.

stories.article_excerpt: In China's fast-moving humanoid robot industry, Shenzhen-based EngineAI has gone from founding to factory floor to IPO candidate in under three years.

stories.headline: Anthropic and OpenAI's Revenue-Accounting Split Becomes an IPO Flashpoint
stories.key_takeaway: Anthropic books cloud-partner sales as gross revenue while OpenAI nets them out — a gap worth billions that the SEC may force to reconcile before either company can go public.

pull_quote.text: If they both IPO in the coming quarters, not sure how the SEC is going to let these two companies have different accounting treatment for essentially the same type of revenue.

stories.article_excerpt: Two of Silicon Valley's most valuable private companies are racing toward public markets — and can't agree on how to count their revenue.

stories.headline: Anthropic Is Blindsiding Its Own Business Partners, The Information Reports
stories.key_takeaway: A new report says Anthropic has repeatedly launched products that compete with its own partners with little warning and changed enterprise pricing overnight, raising trust questions ahead of a potential Q4 2026 IPO.

pull_quote.text: costs for heavy users could double or triple

data_points.label: User threshold below which pricing change didn't apply
Estimated cost jump for heavy users
Anthropic commitment to its Partner Network

stories.article_excerpt: A new report from The Information paints an uncomfortable portrait of Anthropic's partner relations: a pattern of surprise competitive launches.

stories.headline: IBM and ServiceNow Expand Their Alliance to Run Agentic AI on Enterprises' Legacy Systems
stories.key_takeaway: IBM and ServiceNow's June 11 collaboration layers IBM's data and automation tools onto the ServiceNow AI Platform so enterprises can run agentic AI on legacy systems without replacing them.

pull_quote.text: Most enterprises have the ambition to deploy agentic AI, but lack the foundation to run it at scale. IBM brings the tooling to modernize the systems; ServiceNow provides the platform to put that data to work across every workflow in the business.
pull_quote.role: SVP and GM, Central Product Management, ServiceNow

stories.article_excerpt: For the world's largest enterprises, the biggest obstacle to AI at scale is not a shortage of models — it is decades of legacy infrastructure.

stories.headline: A Leaked 'GPT-5.6' Checkpoint Is Circulating — Stronger Vision, Bigger Context, No Official Word
stories.key_takeaway: An unreleased GPT-5.6 'kindle-alpha' checkpoint has surfaced via Codex testing paths with unconfirmed reports of a ~1.5M-token context and sharper vision — but OpenAI has announced nothing, so treat every claim as a field report.

pull_quote.text: Unreleased GPT-5.6 checkpoints show a marked jump in vision — long the GPT-5.x weak spot. Sharper SVG output has testers calling the model ahead of Gemini.
pull_quote.role: AI news publication covering the leak

data_points.label: Polymarket odds of release by June 30

stories.article_excerpt: A checkpoint identified as GPT-5.6 'kindle-alpha' began circulating in developer communities in early June, surfacing through Codex testing paths.

stories.headline: Alibaba's Qwen 3.7 Plus Folds Vision, Language and Coding Into One Multimodal Model
stories.key_takeaway: Qwen3.7-Plus unifies vision, video and coding in one agent model at $0.40 per million input tokens — a strategic pivot from the open-weight releases that built Qwen's reputation toward a low-cost, API-only product.

pull_quote.text: Today we introduce Qwen3.7-Plus, a multimodal agent model that unifies vision and language into a single, versatile agent foundation.

stories.article_excerpt: While Microsoft Build 2026 commanded the stage in Redmond, Alibaba quietly dropped one of the most economically consequential model releases.

stories.headline: Claude Code Gains a '/fork' Command and a New CLI for Branching AI Coding Sessions
stories.key_takeaway: Anthropic's new /fork command lets developers branch an active Claude Code session into parallel variants — borrowing git's branching model — while a new CLI deepens toolchain integration atop the earlier nested sub-agents feature.

pull_quote.text: Most Claude Code users face the same problem: two possible approaches, no way to know which one Claude will execute better, so they pick one and hope.

data_points.label: Public GitHub commits via Claude Code

stories.article_excerpt: Anthropic has given its coding tool a feature that sounds small and changes the workflow more than its size suggests: session forking.

stories.headline: D-I-TASSER Marries AI With Physics to Predict Tough Protein Structures More Accurately
stories.key_takeaway: D-I-TASSER combines deep learning with physics-based simulation to predict multi-domain protein structures about 13% more accurately than prior tools, opening new avenues for hard drug-discovery targets.

pull_quote.text: For most proteins, we still do not know their 3D structures, and that remains a major blind spot in biology. A protein's shape determines what it does in the body, but many large, multi-domain proteins are too complex for existing tools to model reliably.

stories.article_excerpt: For years, researchers hailed AlphaFold as the answer to biology's protein-folding challenge. A new hybrid tool targets where it still struggles.

stories.headline: Google Researchers Ask When Multi-Agent AI Actually Helps — and When It Backfires
stories.key_takeaway: New Google research finds multi-agent AI can lift performance dramatically on parallelizable tasks but degrade it on sequential ones, making architecture-task fit more important than simply adding more agents.

pull_quote.text: While performance generally trends upward with more capable models, multi-agent systems are not a universal solution — they can either significantly boost or unexpectedly degrade performance depending on the specific configuration.

stories.article_excerpt: The AI industry has been racing to stack agents. New research from Google challenges that bet with hard numbers about when it fails.

stories.headline: Study Finds Large Reasoning Models Can Act as Autonomous Jailbreak Agents
stories.key_takeaway: Researchers show a large reasoning model can be turned into a fully autonomous jailbreak agent that defeats nine frontier models 97% of the time from a single system prompt — evidence of a systemic 'alignment regression.'

pull_quote.text: As LRMs become more capable in reasoning and strategizing, they also become more competent at subverting alignment in other models. This feedback loop, if left unaddressed, could degrade the security posture of the entire model ecosystem.

stories.article_excerpt: A new peer-reviewed study documents something AI-safety researchers have long feared: a capable reasoning model that can jailbreak others on its own.

stories.headline: Robin, an AI 'Scientist' Agent, Runs the Drug-Discovery Loop From Hypothesis to Analysis
stories.key_takeaway: FutureHouse's Robin multi-agent system ran the full discovery loop — reading 551 papers in 30 minutes, forming hypotheses, and surfacing ripasudil as a candidate for dry AMD — in work published in Nature.

pull_quote.text: By integrating these agents into a unified system, we've been able to automate the key intellectual steps of the entire scientific process and achieve our first AI-generated discovery.

stories.article_excerpt: AI in drug discovery has mostly meant point solutions. Robin tries to automate the whole loop — and produced its first candidate molecule.

stories.headline: CNN Sues Perplexity as the Publisher-vs-AI Copyright War Widens to Nine Plaintiffs
stories.key_takeaway: CNN's copyright and trademark suit against Perplexity — the ninth active case against the startup — could reshape the economics of AI 'answer engines' if courts rule that real-time synthesis of news needs a license.

pull_quote.text: Commercial operators can and must pay to make use of it. We prefer that they do so through sensible licensing arrangements, but if they refuse, they will have to pay through legal damages. There is no free option.

data_points.label: AI copyright cases pending in U.S. courts

stories.article_excerpt: The lawsuit the AI industry had been bracing for arrived on May 28, when CNN filed a federal complaint against the 'answer engine.'

stories.headline: Europe Tightens the Screws on AI Training Data: A 250M Euro Google Fine and a German Order Against OpenAI
stories.key_takeaway: European courts and regulators have moved from warning to enforcement on AI training data — a 250M euro fine against Google and a German order forcing OpenAI to purge stored song lyrics are reshaping licensing strategy across the continent.

pull_quote.text: The Autorite considers that Google has breached its commitment by failing to inform publishers that their content had been used to train Bard.

data_points.label: Munich ruling in GEMA v. OpenAI
EU AI Act training-data transparency starts

stories.article_excerpt: Europe is not waiting for legal consensus. A fine against Google and a court order against OpenAI mark a shift from warnings to enforcement.

stories.headline: The EU AI Act's August Deadline Looms: What General-Purpose AI Providers Must Soon Disclose
stories.key_takeaway: On August 2, 2026, the EU AI Office gains full enforcement power over GPAI providers, who must already publish training-data summaries and copyright policies or risk fines up to 15M euro or 3% of global turnover.

pull_quote.text: This Code introduces a number of legal uncertainties for model developers, as well as measures which go far beyond the scope of the AI Act.

data_points.label: EU AI Office gains GPAI enforcement power
Max GPAI fine (euro or global turnover)

stories.article_excerpt: Seven weeks out, on August 2, 2026, the EU AI Act's enforcement regime clicks into its next gear for general-purpose model providers.
